commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=af895ccaf7205b26525c34d862531c487007cc17)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `VOX_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS`, per-query HTTP/subprocess agent timeouts, `query_agent` error recovery, sanitized HTTP agent failures, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. On current main, a `VOX_AGENT_CMD` process that never writes a JSONL response, or an HTTP agent that never responds, leaves `handleResponseDone` awaiting `opts.agent.query` without a timeout or recovery path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer judgment on upgrade compatibility, not a safe automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agent): add configurable timeout for query_agent calls
target.body:
## Summary

- `query_agent` calls (HTTP or subprocess agent) could hang indefinitely if the agent never responds, leaving the call stuck.
- Adds `VOX_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS` (default `10000`) to bound every `query_agent` call.
- On timeout (or any other agent error), `handleResponseDone` now sends an error `function_call_output` plus a `response.create` so the model can recover and tell the caller something went wrong, instead of leaving the conversation hanging.
- `createHttpAgentClient` previously shared a single `AbortController` across all queries on a client; once one query timed out and aborted it, every subsequent query on that client would fail immediately. Reworked to use a per-query `AbortController`, so a timeout on one `query_agent` call doesn't break later calls in the same conversation.
- `createSubprocessAgentClient` gets a per-request timer that rejects and cleans up the pending map entry without killing the child process.
- **Sanitization (addresses review feedback):** non-OK HTTP agent responses previously sent their raw response body straight to the model in `function_call_output`. `AgentError` now separates a generic model-facing `message` ("Agent request failed") from a `detail` string that's written only to the call's `events.jsonl` log, so internal error bodies (stack traces, connection strings, etc.) never reach the model or caller.

## Real behavior proof

Ran the actual `vox serve` process against a fake Realtime backend (`ws://127.0.0.1:8083`, a small `ws` stand-in) and a real Twilio-shaped caller socket, with two scenarios:

**Scenario A — stalled subprocess agent (`VOX_AGENT_CMD='node -e "process.stdin.resume()"'`, `VOX_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS=2000`)**

Realtime stand-in log:
```
12:46:24.417Z provider: <- session.update {...}
12:46:24.719Z provider: -> response.done (query_agent function_call)
12:46:26.721Z provider: <- conversation.item.create {"type":"conversation.item.create","item":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_timeout_demo","output":"{\"ok\":false,\"error\":\"Agent query timed out after 2000ms\"}"}}
12:46:26.721Z provider: <- response.create {"type":"response.create"}
```
`events.jsonl`:
```json
{"t":"2026-06-15T12:46:26.721Z","source":"vox","payload":{"type":"tool.error","error":"Agent query timed out after 2000ms"}}
```
The query timed out after ~2s (as configured) and the model received both the error output and a follow-up `response.create` — the call kept going instead of hanging.

**Scenario B — broken HTTP agent (`VOX_AGENT_URL` pointing at a server returning `500` with a body containing a fake DB connection string)**

Realtime stand-in log:
```
12:47:22.591Z provider: -> response.done (query_agent function_call)
12:47:22.620Z provider: <- conversation.item.create {"type":"conversation.item.create","item":{"type":"function_call_output","call_id":"call_timeout_demo","output":"{\"ok\":false,\"error\":\"Agent request failed\"}"}}
12:47:22.620Z provider: <- response.create {"type":"response.create"}
```
`events.jsonl`:
```json
{"t":"2026-06-15T12:47:22.620Z","source":"vox","payload":{"type":"tool.error","error":"Agent HTTP 500: internal error: db connection string postgres://user:secret123@db.internal/prod"}}
```
The model only ever sees `"Agent request failed"` — the response body (including the fake credential) is confined to the server-side `events.jsonl` log.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm run lint`
- [x] `pnpm run typecheck`
- [x] `pnpm test` — 53/53 passing, including:
  - `test/server-agent-timeout.test.ts` — stalled subprocess agent produces an error `function_call_output` + `response.create`; HTTP agent failure sends a generic error to the model while logging the full response body
  - `test/agent.test.ts` — subprocess timeout, HTTP timeout, HTTP client recovers on the next query after a prior timeout, and HTTP failures are surfaced via `AgentError` with a sanitized message
  - `test/config.test.ts` — `VOX_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS` defaults/parses/validates
- [x] `pnpm run build`

## Notes for maintainers

- The `10000`ms default timeout changes existing behavior for installs relying on (previously) unbounded agent calls. Flagging this for explicit sign-off — happy to adjust the default or add a way to opt out of the timeout if that's preferred.
target.author: joevenner

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
